answer:
The Notion API authenticates requests using bearer tokens passed in the HTTP Authorization header [1]. Whether using an internal connection, a personal access token (PAT), or an OAuth-based public connection, you must include this header in every REST API request [1][2].

For manual HTTP requests, provide the token as shown below [1][3]:

```http
Authorization: Bearer {YOUR_TOKEN}
Notion-Version: 2026-03-11
```

If using the Notion SDK for JavaScript, initialize the client with your token once [1][4]:

```javascript
const { Client } = require('@notionhq/client');
const client = new Client({ auth: process.env.NOTION_ACCESS_TOKEN });
```

Public connections require an initial OAuth 2.0 flow to exchange a temporary code for an access token [2][5]. Internal connections and PATs use static tokens generated via the Notion developer portal [2][6].
